page: Microsoft Azure B16s_v2 is a burstable virtual machine instance belonging to the Bsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Performance benchmarks place the B16s_v2 in the average tier across most standardized tests. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance scored 1899.28, while the multi-core CPU test reached 15526.19. In memory operations, the instance achieved a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 839.5 GB/sec and an uncached large block write bandwidth of 52.68 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the server demonstrated a strong tier result in LLM text generation using a small 135M model, reaching 261.17 tokens/sec, while its medium 7B model text generation speed sat at an average 12.34 tokens/sec.

The burstable CPU allocation model offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads with fluctuating resource demands, allowing users to pay less during idle periods while retaining the ability to burst to full CPU capacity when required. However, the lack of local storage and dedicated GPU hardware represents a clear tradeoff, requiring external storage configuration for persistent data. This profile is suitable for general-purpose workloads, development environments, build servers, and low-to-medium traffic web serving where continuous maximum CPU utilization is not required.
description: Microsoft Azure B16s_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Bsv2 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It provides 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores, 64.0 GB of RAM, and operates on the x86_64 architecture via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features no local storage or GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate average performance across most metrics, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 9893.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 21253.65, with a strong result in small-model LLM text generation at 261.17 tokens/sec. Due to its burstable CPU allocation, the B16s_v2 offers a cost-effective solution for workloads with variable resource demands, such as development servers, code compilation, and light database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B16s_v2 is a burstable virtual machine featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and no local storage. It is designed for cost-effective, variable workloads like development and light database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B16s_v2 is a burstable x86_64 virtual machine with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, designed for variable general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A burstable x86_64 virtual machine with sixteen vCPUs and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for cost-effective handling of variable workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 64.0 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor
Burstable CPU allocation model for cost-effective variable workloads
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage or GPU
Average performance in standard CPU, memory, and database benchmarks
Strong text generation speed of 261.17 tokens/sec on small LLM models
